views.faq_des:
If you have never invested with tokens, you may have questions about how they work. These are the questions we are asked most often. However, it is important to clarify that the token is merely a tool that provides the investor with greater liquidity and represents an investment in an underlying asset, the real estate projects.



What is Blockchain?


Blockchain is a technology based on a chain of blocks with a public and distributed database in which transactions carried out on the network are securely recorded.

This decentralized database works like a ledger that cannot be modified, and requires tens of thousands of computers that automatically share and validate the transactions made within that ledger.

To carry out any transaction, we need an intermediary to verify who we are and the operation we are going to perform to ensure it is secure.

When you make a bank transfer or a digital payment, there is one or more banks that you trust to carry out the transaction and validate the operation. Banks store and use that information and our data.

The Blockchain network eliminates those intermediaries. Because the network itself, with its users, validates those transactions. Each participant keeps a copy and validates each transaction that is executed. A centralized body is no longer needed to say that an operation is correct; the community does it.

Blockchain technology provides a maximum level of security for transactions between people or entities, as it stores said transactions in a temporal and immutable record (chain of blocks) (it cannot be manipulated), distributed across multiple independent nodes (it cannot be censored or controlled by a central entity), transparent (the transaction record is public) and confidential (the identity of the parties is not revealed, for greater privacy and security).



What is a Token?


A token is the digital representation of an asset with value or utility, which is registered on the Blockchain network. Through various methods, any asset can be represented with a token; in our case, real estate projects.

The registration of the token on the Blockchain network ensures the transaction history through the chain of blocks.

At Stellar, we divide the financing needs of a project into tokens that represent a proportional part of that financing need. That token generates economic rights against the project.



What is tokenization?


Asset tokenization is the process through which tokens are generated (which digitally represent the securities or rights that a natural or legal person can legally issue).

There are many ways to launch tokens, some regulated and others not. We use STOs (Security Token Offering), because it is the one that offers the investor the greatest security and legal backing, which for us is the most important thing. We need a community that feels supported.



What is an STO?


An STO (Security Token Offering) consists of the process of issuing and distributing to investors tokens that grant economic rights over the company that issues them, whether in the form of equity or debt. The important thing is that they are backed by real estate projects... And at the same time, regulated by the CNMV.



What are the advantages of tokenization?


One of the foundations of tokenization is collaborative investment. It allows all types of investors to access opportunities that were previously unavailable to them due to insufficient financial capacity or the need for diversification.

Additionally, it increases their liquidity. A secondary market is created where tokens can be bought and sold.

Tokenized investment allows access to opportunities that generate profitability, which depending on the project can be secure and with greater liquidity. Additionally, it enables portfolio diversification.

Geographic barriers do not exist, investments are transparent and traceable, and the investor acquires total control over their investment.



What do we tokenize at Stellar?


We tokenize real estate projects. Each token represents a portion of the economic rights of that project. By buying a token, the investor is actually buying a part of the project.

The asset is tokenized but not directly. A hotel is not tokenized (the token is not 45 m2 of the building). A contract is tokenized, a company that owns that hotel. Each token represents a part of the company or economic rights over it, which obtains its returns from the ownership and operation of the asset.



How are tokens traded?


Stellar is a platform for the launch, promotion and buying and selling of tokens. An ecosystem for all those who are already interested in this world of tokenized investment, or all those who want to enter it.

Within the platform, there are two fundamental moments where it is possible to trade tokens: the primary market and the secondary market



What is the primary token market?


At the time of the issuance and launch (what we call a Drop) of a tokenized project, investors who have completed the registration process will be able to purchase tokens. This direct purchase from the token issuer is called the primary market.



What is the secondary market?


Suppose that in the primary market you fail to acquire any tokens from the project you are interested in. This is when the secondary market comes into play, a place where people who have acquired tokens from tokenized projects in the primary market can trade with them, putting them up for sale again. In turn, new buyers can choose to sell them a second time...
views.community:
We want to create an exclusive investment community, and therefore, we consider it necessary to establish some key rules and expectations to begin with. We hope you can understand these ideas and help us work to build an accessible and international community.



For the community


To break with the old and start something truly new, we must be guided by values of kindness and inclusion. Showing mutual respect and care is of utmost importance to all of us as we build a community that welcomes everyone.



For investors


Welcome to this new way of investing. With us you will find high-quality international Real Estate opportunities. Nevertheless, we like to promote responsible investment. Diversification is very important to increase portfolio security. You can diversify with projects in Spain and projects in Mexico or different projects in the same country. Additionally, it is important to diversify in other sectors.



When you make a Token purchase on STELLAR:


You own the acquired Tokens that represent the rights linked to them, through a Smart Contract on Blockchain.


You can sell the Token (if it is transferable).

We all have the responsibility to create a positive environment

Do not lie, spam, steal, cause harm or promote hate. If you observe any of these behaviors on the platform, please let us know through our support email soporte{'@'}stellar.es. If we determine that a user is engaging in any of the mentioned behaviors, we will take action on the situation, which may lead to account suspension. Full details can be found in our Terms of Service.
views.legal:
This document contains both the basic information of Stellar, S.L and the website https://stellar.es/ (Legal Notice), as well as the terms of use and access to it (Terms of Use). Both the Legal Notice and the Terms of Use shall be jointly referred to as the "Terms".

Legal Notice

To comply with the requirements of Law 34/2002, of July 11, on Information Society Services and Electronic Commerce, and in particular its Article 10, the following legal notice is made available to users (hereinafter, the "Legal Notice").

Access to, navigation through and use of the website https://stellar.es/ (hereinafter, the "Website") imply tacit and unreserved acceptance of all the provisions of this Legal Notice, having the same validity and effectiveness as any written and signed contract.

Its observance and compliance shall be enforceable with respect to any person who accesses, navigates or uses the Website (hereinafter, the "User" or "Users"). If you do not agree with the stated terms, do not access, navigate or use them.


Identification of the responsible party

In compliance with the duty of information set forth in Article 10 of Law 34/2002, of July 11, on Information Society Services and Electronic Commerce, the following data of the holder are reflected below:

  • Holder: Stellar, S.L. (hereinafter, "STELLAR" or "Holder");
  • Registered office: Calle Ayala, 11, 3, 28001, Madrid.
  • NIF/CIF: B-44555076
  • E-mail: soporte{'@'}stellar.com.

Below, the terms that generally govern access to the Holder's website are presented, both for the services and functionalities offered therein, without prejudice to the application of other different terms for the contracting of goods or services or their modification. A careful reading of these Terms is recommended each time the User accesses the Website, as they may be subject to modifications, which the Holder will publish duly so that the User is aware of them.

In any case, the use of some functionalities of the Website may be subject to specific terms that, depending on the case, replace, complete and/or modify these Terms. Therefore, before using said functionalities, the User must also carefully read and accept the corresponding specific terms.


Purpose of the Website

The purpose of STELLAR is the development of a technology platform (the "Platform") that will serve as a meeting point for, on the one hand, promoters, developers, or owners of real estate projects seeking project financing (the "Promoters") and, on the other hand, Users who wish to invest in said projects (the "Investor Users"). In this case, the investment in the promoted projects is structured through the issuance of financial instruments in the form of tokens, such as participative loans.


User obligations

As a User, by merely accessing and navigating through the Website, you must:

  1. Use the Website diligently, correctly and lawfully, always respecting current legislation, morality and good customs, as well as public order.
  2. Periodically review these Terms, or any others that may apply, checking for any modifications that may occur.
  3. Review the communications that STELLAR sends you, as they may contain important information.
  4. Not use the Website for commercial purposes, especially to collect information or content for the purpose of providing services that clearly constitute competition for STELLAR.
  5. Not modify or attempt to modify in any way, or carry out actions or use means aimed at simulating the appearance or functions of the Website.
  6. Not damage, disable, overload or hinder the service (or the network or networks connected to the service), or interfere with its use and enjoyment.
  7. Refrain from carrying out any action that involves the introduction of computer viruses, worms, trojans or any other type of malicious code intended to interrupt, destroy or limit the functionalities of the Website.
  8. Not employ reverse engineering techniques and/or decrypt, decompile or use any other system designed to learn the source code of STELLAR.
  9. In any case, not carry out any type of act that may violate the rights or interests of the Holder or third parties, such as, by way of example, intellectual or industrial property rights (patents, trademarks, copyright, trade secrets...).

Website availability

The Holder strives to improve and expand STELLAR, as well as its content and the services offered therein. Despite this, it reserves the right to modify, limit or cancel said elements when deemed appropriate, as well as to limit, deny or restrict access to Users, temporarily or permanently, without prior notice and for any reason, especially in the event that the User fails to comply with these Terms. Likewise, the Holder is not responsible for any type of damage resulting from the lack of availability or continuity of the Website and the services offered therein, although it will make every effort to ensure, to the extent possible, the continuity and proper functioning of the Website.


Registration as a User
  1. Registration and User account

    To access the full content of the Platform, the User must register on the Website. Only natural persons of legal age may register as Users. Users who are minors, incapacitated, or have their capacity complemented may NOT register as Users under any circumstances. In the case of legal entities, only their legal representative may register on their behalf, accrediting their authority to act on behalf of the company or entity in question (through power of attorney or other documents).

    The User must access the "Log In" option at the top right of the website and then click on "Register" to complete the Email, password and repeat password fields and finally click "Register". All the steps indicated must be completed, including acceptance of the Privacy Policy and this document.

    STELLAR will confirm the User's registration on the Website by sending a welcome communication, from which point they become a "Registered User".

    If the Registered User does not remember their password, they must click on "I forgot my password" and enter the email address through which they registered to reset it. The Holder reserves the right to verify and validate the User's identity at any time. Failure to comply with the requirements set out in this section or deception regarding identity authorizes the Holder to deregister such User at any time, exempting the Holder from any liability for such action.

  2. Requirements for investing in any of the Website's projects

    The User, once inside the private area of the Website, may invest in any of the available real estate projects. To do so, the User must first pass a KYC ("Know Your Customer") process. The Holder has entered into an agreement with a third-party entity responsible for managing the payment accounts of each Registered User who wishes to invest in any of the Website's projects, through which said entity will be responsible for (i) verifying KYC data, (ii) opening payment accounts for each User who wishes to invest, and (iii) managing the payment system for each project (money-in, money-out, receipt of recurring returns, etc.).

    In the event that the User wishes to invest in a tokenized project, they must pay the indicated amount in fiat money through the payment methods established in each case by the tokenized project.

  3. Rights and obligations of the Registered User
    1. Access the private area through their credentials.
    2. Under no circumstances, facilitate or share their access credentials to a third party, without authorization or consent.
    3. Be solely responsible for all activities carried out from their personal account on STELLAR.
    4. Take care of the confidentiality of their access data, as they will be responsible for any damage suffered by themselves or third parties for non-compliance with these Terms. They will also be responsible for what happens in their personal account unless its security has been compromised by external causes. Specifically, they must:
      1. Keep the account updated.
      2. Store the chosen password confidentially.
      3. Be the only User using the created account.
      4. Not market, sell or transfer the account to a third party.
    5. STELLAR reserves the right to verify the Registered Investor User's profile, being able to delete or suspend the account in case of non-compliance with these legal texts or any others applicable.
    6. By virtue of the above, it is the User's obligation to immediately notify STELLAR of any fact that allows the improper use of identifiers and/or passwords, such as theft, loss or unauthorized access to them, in order to proceed with their immediate cancellation. Until such facts are communicated, STELLAR shall be exempt from any liability that may arise from the improper use of identifiers or passwords by unauthorized third parties.

Duration

Access as a Registered User will begin once the required data has been entered to complete registration, after acceptance of the Privacy Policy and these Terms.

Access as a Registered User will remain in force indefinitely until the User notifies STELLAR of their deregistration by email to: soporte{'@'}stellar.com.


Exclusion of liability
  1. Liability for Website availability

    The Holder will make every effort to ensure, to the extent possible, the continuity and proper functioning of the Website and its content. However, it does not guarantee said availability or that its content or service will be free of errors or that they will be corrected. Nor can it guarantee that the content available through the Website is free of viruses or other harmful components. The Holder does not grant any warranty, either express or implied, including but not limited to, warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, non-infringement of intellectual property or other rights.

  2. Liability for third-party links

    The Platform may include links to third-party pages or sites unrelated to the Holder, over which it assumes no responsibility as it does not approve or review their functions and content. The User who accesses any link published on the Website does so at their own risk and venture, without the Holder assuming any responsibility for this.

  3. Liability for misuse of the Website

    Likewise, the Website is exempt from all liability arising from misuse of it by Users, as well as from non-compliance with obligations or commitments assumed by virtue of these terms or any others applicable.


Intellectual and industrial property rights

All intellectual and industrial property rights over the Website and its contents, including programming, design, applications, graphics, codes, text or images, trademarks, trade names, distinctive signs and other elements present therein belong exclusively to the Holder, including the STELLAR trademark, or it has sufficient rights and/or authorizations for their exploitation. As a consequence of the above, any reproduction, distribution, public communication (including making available), transformation or any other form of exploitation, even citing the sources, is prohibited unless prior, express and written consent of the Holder or the exclusive holder of the affected rights has been obtained. If you detect any infringement, please report it to the email address indicated in the header.


Indemnification

The Holder may sanction any User who fails to comply with the applicable terms, by making it impossible, temporarily or indefinitely, to access the Website. The duration of the sanction will depend on the type of infraction committed in each case. These sanctions do not exclude any other actions that the Holder may take against the User in any order. The User undertakes to indemnify the Holder and hold it harmless from and against any claim, damage or demand (including reasonable attorneys' fees and costs) made by any third party as a result of a breach of any provision of these Terms by the User.


Applicable law and jurisdiction

These Terms shall be governed by Spanish law. In the event of any controversy, discrepancy, question or claim, the parties submit themselves to the courts and tribunals of Madrid, expressly waiving any other jurisdiction that may correspond to them.


Product Terms

The Products offered through this Website (the "Products") correspond to the digital tokens emitted on the Blockchain network that represent certain economic rights on each project promoted by the Promoters.

For the acquisition of the Products it is essential to be a Registered User and have passed the KYC process. Each project may have different investment conditions.

The price of each Token will be determined within each project and will be available for the User to consult on the project page.

Payments may be made through the payment methods available on the Website (bank transfer, credit card or crypto wallets). Each payment must comply with the conditions established in each project.

Applicable taxes shall be determined in each case in accordance with the tax legislation in force.


Right of withdrawal

The User has the right to withdraw from the acquisition of Products without the need for justification, within a period of 14 calendar days from the purchase date, provided that the tokens have not been transferred to the Blockchain or the project has not been closed.

To exercise the right of withdrawal, the User must notify STELLAR by email to soporte{'@'}stellar.com. STELLAR will proceed to refund the investment made using the same payment method used by the User, within a maximum period of 14 calendar days from the date on which it was informed of the withdrawal decision.


Modification of the Terms

STELLAR reserves the right to modify these Terms at any time, publishing the updated version on the Website. The modifications will not apply retroactively. If the User does not agree with the new Terms, they must stop using the Website. Continued use of the Website after the publication of the modified Terms shall constitute acceptance thereof. In any case, the Terms in force at the time of contracting the Products shall apply to purchases in progress.


Suspension and termination

Non-compliance with any of the above obligations by the User may lead to the adoption by STELLAR of the appropriate measures protected by Law and in the exercise of its rights or obligations, which may include the deletion or blocking of the infringing User's account so that they cannot acquire the Products, without any possibility of compensation for damages caused.

STELLAR reserves the possibility to alter, modify or suspend any feature of the Website.


Privacy and data protection

In accordance with the provisions of Regulation 2016/679 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 27 April 2016 on the protection of natural persons with regard to the processing of personal data and on the free movement of such data (General Data Protection Regulation) and Organic Law 3/2018, of December 5, on the Protection of Personal Data and guarantee of digital rights, all personal data collected during the use of the Website will be processed in accordance with the Privacy Policy, which every User must expressly accept when providing personal data through the private area of the Website.


Contact

STELLAR makes available to the User the following email address soporte{'@'}stellar.com for the purpose of providing support and assistance, as well as to receive any comments or concerns from the User, both during the service contracting procedures and afterwards.

Additionally, it should be noted that the User has STELLAR's contact details reflected in section 1 of these Terms.


General

The headings of the various clauses are for informational purposes only and shall not affect, qualify or extend the interpretation of these Terms. In the event that any provision of these Terms is declared null or unenforceable, it shall be limited or eliminated to the minimum extent necessary so that these Terms otherwise remain in full force and effect. Failure by STELLAR to enforce any right or provision of these Terms shall not be considered a waiver of said right or provision. STELLAR's relationship with the User shall not be that of a joint venture, partnership, employment or agency, and neither party is authorized to bind the other in any way. These Terms and the information and documents referred to in them constitute the entire agreement between the User and STELLAR regarding the purpose of these Terms and replace any other previous agreement. If, during access to the Website and finalization of these Terms, the User identifies any error when entering their data, they must contact STELLAR through the contact form or email address. STELLAR will provide the necessary support and, where appropriate, correct the identified errors.

Unless otherwise provided in these Terms, notifications between the User and STELLAR must be made in writing by email to the corresponding addresses. For these purposes, STELLAR's contact details shall be those appearing in these Terms and the User's contact details shall be those established on the Website.


Complaints system

If the User considers that any of their rights has been violated, STELLAR proposes that they communicate this through the email soporte{'@'}stellar.com provided for this purpose. STELLAR commits to respond to and address their concerns regarding the alleged infringement of rights.

Once all the information has been received, STELLAR may take the necessary measures with the aim of investigating and resolving the situation.

In no case shall the implementation of any measure mean the admission by STELLAR of any liability. STELLAR reserves the exercise of any right, action or defense available to it.


Assignment

The User may not assign this contract (in whole or in part) without the prior written consent of STELLAR. However, STELLAR may assign all or part of this Contract to any entity that may take over the provision of the Services included in the Website in the future.


Language

The language applicable to these Terms is Spanish. If versions in other languages such as English are provided, it shall be solely as a courtesy and for the User's convenience. In case of contradiction, the Spanish version shall prevail.


Online Dispute Resolution

The European Commission provides an online dispute resolution platform, available at the following link: http://ec.europa.eu/consumers/odr/.

Last updated: February 10, 2023
views.policy:
Below, we inform you about the Privacy Policy that will apply in those cases in which the data subject, through browsing the website https://stellar.es/ (hereinafter, the "Website"), provides us with their personal data. We want to inform you of our updated Privacy Policy and the purpose given to the personal data you provide us, including those we collect by virtue of browsing our Website.


  1. IDENTIFICATION OF THE CONTROLLER
  • Holder: Stellar, S.L. (hereinafter, "STELLAR" or "Holder");
  • Registered office: Calle Ayala, 11, 3, 28001, Madrid.
  • NIF/CIF: B-44555076
  • E-mail: soporte{'@'}stellar.com.

  1. INFORMATION AND CONSENT

In accordance with the provisions of Regulation (EU) 679/2016 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 27 April 2016 on the protection of natural persons with regard to the processing of personal data and on the free movement of such data (hereinafter, "GDPR") and Organic Law 3/2018, of December 5, on the Protection of Personal Data and Guarantee of Digital Rights (hereinafter, "LOPDGDD"), by accepting this Privacy Policy the web data subject (hereinafter, the "Data Subject") GIVES THEIR CONSENT, informed, express, free and unequivocal, for the processing of personal data collected through the Website, whether through forms enabled for this purpose or through the information collected by cookies.

Likewise, the Data Subject consents to their data being processed by the Controller to provide its services, applying the organizational and security measures required by the GDPR.

All personal data requested through the Website are mandatory, as they are necessary to provide an optimal service to the Data Subject. In the event that all the data is not provided, STELLAR does not guarantee that the information and services provided will be completely tailored to the Data Subject's needs.


  1. PURPOSE OF THE PROCESSING

The personal data provided through the Website may be processed for the following purposes:

  • Managing the User's registration on the Platform.
  • Managing User identification through the KYC process.
  • Managing the investment process in the projects available on the Platform.
  • Managing the payment of products or services contracted through the Website.
  • Sending commercial communications about products and services offered by the Holder that may be of interest to the Data Subject, provided the Data Subject has given express consent.
  • Responding to queries, complaints or requests made by the Data Subject through the contact channels available on the Website.
  • Complying with the legal obligations applicable to the Holder.

  1. LEGAL BASIS FOR PROCESSING

The legal basis for the processing of personal data is as follows:

  • The Data Subject's consent, expressed through the acceptance of this Privacy Policy.
  • The execution of the contractual relationship between the Holder and the Data Subject.
  • Compliance with the legal obligations applicable to the Holder.
  • The legitimate interest of the Holder, in those cases where the processing is necessary for the satisfaction of legitimate interests pursued by the Holder or by a third party, provided that such interests do not override the interests or fundamental rights and freedoms of the Data Subject.

  1. DATA RETENTION PERIOD

Personal data will be retained for as long as the contractual and/or commercial relationship is maintained with the Data Subject, or until the Data Subject requests their deletion, and in any case for the legally established periods.

Once the data is no longer necessary for the purpose for which it was collected, it will be duly blocked, without prejudice to making it available to the competent public administrations, judges and courts, or the Public Prosecutor's Office, during the statute of limitations of any actions that may arise from the relationship with the Data Subject and/or the legally established retention periods.


  1. DATA RECIPIENTS

The Holder may share the Data Subject's personal data with:

  • Third-party entities that provide services to the Holder, such as payment management companies, identity verification providers, technology service providers, and other companies that collaborate in the provision of the Holder's services.
  • Competent authorities, courts and public administrations, when required by legal obligation.

In any case, the Holder will ensure that these third parties provide sufficient guarantees to implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to ensure that the processing meets the requirements of the GDPR and protects the rights of Data Subjects.


  1. INTERNATIONAL DATA TRANSFERS

The Holder may transfer personal data outside the European Economic Area (EEA) only when necessary and with appropriate safeguards, such as standard contractual clauses approved by the European Commission or adequacy decisions.


  1. DATA SUBJECT RIGHTS

The Data Subject may exercise the following rights:

  • Right of access: to know whether or not personal data is being processed and, where applicable, to obtain a copy thereof.
  • Right of rectification: to request the correction of inaccurate data or to complete incomplete data.
  • Right of erasure: to request the deletion of personal data when, among other reasons, the data is no longer necessary for the purposes for which it was collected.
  • Right to restriction of processing: to request the limitation of data processing in certain cases, in which case only the data necessary for the exercise or defense of claims will be retained.
  • Right to data portability: to receive the personal data provided to the Controller in a structured, commonly used and machine-readable format, and to transmit it to another controller.
  • Right to object: to object to the processing of data when it is based on the legitimate interest of the Controller.
  • Right to withdraw consent: at any time, without affecting the lawfulness of the processing based on consent prior to its withdrawal.

To exercise these rights, the Data Subject must send a written communication to the email address soporte{'@'}stellar.com, including their identification and specifying the right they wish to exercise.

Likewise, the Data Subject may file a complaint with the Spanish Data Protection Agency (www.aepd.es) if they consider that their rights have been violated.


  1. SECURITY MEASURES

The Holder has adopted the necessary technical and organizational measures to guarantee the security and integrity of personal data, and to prevent its alteration, loss, unauthorized processing or access, taking into account the state of technology, the nature of the stored data and the risks to which they are exposed.


  1. MODIFICATION OF THE PRIVACY POLICY

The Holder reserves the right to modify this Privacy Policy at any time, in accordance with the legislation in force at any given time. Any modification will be duly published on the Website. For this reason, it is recommended that the Data Subject review this policy periodically.

Last updated: February 10, 2023
